campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help local youth through recreation scholarships. They also discussed extending a temporary local rule (ordinance 2326) and moved forward on plans for a new bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including submitting grant applications to help pay for it.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation to youth recreation scholarships from Water Tower Kitchen
Extension of interim ordinance 2326 (temporary local rule)
New bicycle and pedestrian bridge project over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application for bridge planning and feasibility study

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council introduced an ordinance (a new city law) and skipped the normal first reading step to speed up the process. The council directed staff to put this ordinance on the consent calendar so it can be officially adopted at the next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked (first reading waived)
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption at next council meeting
Specific topic of ordinance not detailed in agenda summary

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve records from several previous meetings held in March and to authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The meeting focused on administrative housekeeping and moving forward with a construction project, though the specific details of the construction work are not fully outlined in the available agenda.
keyTopics: Approval of March meeting minutes from multiple sessions
Authorization of construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given signing authority for the contract

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 21, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José City Council approved several important agreements and projects on April 21, 2026. The main items included upgrades to the airport's baggage system, new protections for affordable housing if subsidies end, updates to stormwater drain planning, and moves forward on a family camp project with FEMA funding.
keyTopics: Airport baggage conveyor system upgrade contract
Affordable housing protections if subsidies expire
Storm sewer system master planning update
FEMA-funded family camp project

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The City Council held a planning meeting on April 14, 2026 to review Mountain View's upcoming spending plans for capital projects like infrastructure improvements. They also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and community volunteers in the city.
keyTopics: Budget review for capital projects in fiscal year 2026-27
Teen Month recognition and celebration
National Volunteer Week proclamation
Infrastructure and improvement spending plans

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held their regular meeting on April 7, 2026, and took time to recognize Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. The meeting began with the traditional flag salute and included these special observances as part of their agenda.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
Regular City Council meeting proceedings

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a community workshop on April 13, 2026 to help residents understand and use OpenGov, a tool that shows how the city spends taxpayer money. The council also discussed rules requiring people who lobby the city to register and disclose their activities.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool demo and public access
City budget review and community input opportunity
Lobbyist registration and disclosure requirements under municipal code

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal notices for residents. If you disagree with a city decision (like a permit denial or zoning ruling), you have 90 days from when the decision is final to file a lawsuit challenging it.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to sue the city over decisions
Legal challenges to permits and zoning rulings
California civil procedure requirements
How to protect your legal rights against city decisions

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to discuss updates to the city's federal funding plans for 2025-2026, review requests from council members for new projects and initiatives, and consider changing the process for filling council vacancies. The council also approved meeting minutes and reviewed upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Federal housing and community development funding plan updates
New project requests submitted by individual council members
Changes to how council vacancies are filled
City Council meeting schedule and administrative items

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a public hearing about a historic building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner is asking to officially recognize it as a more important historic resource, which would allow them to build an extra 5,000 square feet of floor space as a reward for preserving and upgrading the building to meet modern earthquake safety standards.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street building gets upgraded historic status
Owner gets 5,000 sq ft bonus construction space for seismic safety improvements
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
Approval of previous board meeting minutes from March
